message:
fcp-mesh: harden lease renew + placement scoring (audit findings)

Two concrete bug fixes from a focused audit of the lease and placement
subsystems, shipped alongside a parallel agent's canonical-active-lease
refactor in acquire().

1. HIGH — LeaseCoordinator::renew() lacked holder/supersession validation.
   The prior signature took no observed-lease slice and blindly minted a
   new expiry from (now_secs + ttl, held_fencing_token) alone. Any caller
   (or any path that stashed a fencing token from a prior observation)
   could extend a lease that had been released, expired, or superseded
   by a higher-token authority transfer. A stale now_secs (NTP step-back,
   clock skew, malicious caller) could also SHORTEN a live lease because
   new_expires replaced the existing expiry unconditionally.

   Fix: renew() now takes &[ObservedLeaseAuthority] and enforces three
   invariants against it:
     (1) requester must still hold an active lease with the claimed
         fencing_token — else Denied{LeaseNotHeld};
     (2) no other active lease for the same subject/purpose may hold
         a higher fencing_token — else Denied{SupersededByPreferredLease};
     (3) monotonic expiry: new_expires = max(now+ttl, held.expires_at)
         so a stale clock can never shorten an active lease.
   The internal caller in acquire() (renewal-as-reacquire path) was
   updated to forward existing_leases.

   Regression tests (coordinator.rs):
     - renew_rejects_when_no_matching_lease
     - renew_rejects_when_lease_superseded
     - renew_rejects_when_held_lease_expired
     - renew_is_monotonic_under_clock_regression
   Existing renew_extends_expiry updated for the new signature.

2. MEDIUM — DevicePreference::SpecificDevice bonus was scaled 100x vs
   other preference variants. The basis-points convention used for
   HighResources divides by 10000; SpecificDevice divided by 100, so
   a max-weight SpecificDevice produced a +100 bonus while HighResources
   at the same weight produced +1. This let any policy-author (legitimate
   or adversarial, wherever untrusted policy could reach the planner)
   force placement onto a chosen node by swamping every other signal —
   resource, locality, latency, version.

   Fix: preference_bonus for SpecificDevice now uses / 10000.0 to match
   basis-points scaling. Commented inline with rationale.

   Regression test (planner.rs): specific_device_bonus_uses_basis_points_scale
   pins the 0.0 / 0.5 / 1.0 boundaries and asserts a 50-point fitness
   gap is not overturned by a single max-weight SpecificDevice bonus.

Phantom-lease-after-crash was the third candidate from the audit but
it's an architectural gap (no lease-reclaim protocol after restart) —
out of scope for a patch pass; left for a dedicated bead.

Verification gap: cargo check/test could not complete in this session —
the machine was contended and repeated builds hit SIGTERM. The renew()
signature change has exactly one non-test caller (acquire(), updated);
renew_extends_expiry was updated for the new arity; the planner fix is
a one-line constant change with a same-file preference_bonus test. A
follow-up run with fcp-mesh --lib --tests is needed to confirm green.

Also includes:
- authority.rs: AuthorityReasonCode::LeaseAcquisitionRejected variant
  (used by parallel agent's capacity-rejection path in acquire()).
- coordinator.rs: canonical_active_leases_for_subject / _count_for_holder
  helpers + AcquireOutcome::Rejected variant (parallel agent's work,
  staged here to ship as a coherent fcp-mesh hardening set).
- multi_node_harness.rs: rustfmt-only reflow of two call sites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
